sentences.en: Sunday. With the seasons changing, Mom declared today "closet clean-out day," a phrase that fills me with mild dread every single year.
sentences.zh: 星期天。隨著換季，媽媽宣布今天是「衣櫃大清理日」，這句話每年都讓我隱隱感到一絲恐懼。

sentences.en: We pulled every piece of clothing out of my closet and sorted it into three piles: keep, donate, and "how did I ever own this."
sentences.zh: 我們把衣櫃裡每一件衣服都拿出來，分成三堆：留下來、捐出去，還有「我到底為什麼會有這件」。

sentences.en: My old raincoat from two years ago barely reached my knees now, which apparently means I have grown more than I realized.
sentences.zh: 我兩年前的舊雨衣現在幾乎只到我膝蓋，顯然代表我長高的幅度比自己以為的還多。

sentences.en: My swimsuit from last summer still fit, so that went straight into the keep pile without any argument.
sentences.zh: 去年夏天的泳衣還穿得下，所以直接進了留下來那堆，完全沒有爭議。

sentences.en: William, meanwhile, refused to give up a sweater three sizes too small, insisting it still "technically" fit if he did not breathe too deeply.
sentences.zh: 同時，William 死不肯放棄一件小了三個尺寸的毛衣，堅持只要他不要呼吸太深，這件衣服「技術上」還算合身。

sentences.en: "That sweater is basically a costume at this point," I told him, and he looked genuinely hurt by that very accurate comment.
sentences.zh: 「這件毛衣現在基本上算是戲服了，」我跟他說，他被這句非常準確的評論傷到，一臉真心受傷。

sentences.en: We also found a pair of ancient slippers that Perry had clearly chewed on at some point in his short, chaotic life.
sentences.zh: 我們還找到一雙古老的拖鞋，明顯在 Perry 短暫又混亂的一生中某個時刻被啃過。

sentences.en: Perry, hearing his own name, wandered over, sniffed the slipper like an old friend, and tried to steal it right out of the donate pile.
sentences.zh: Perry 一聽到自己的名字就晃過來，像對待老朋友一樣聞了聞那隻拖鞋，還想直接從捐出去那堆偷走它。

sentences.en: New clothing shopping came next, and Mom insisted I try everything on in front of the big mirror before we bought anything.
sentences.zh: 接下來是買新衣服的行程，媽媽堅持要我在買之前，先站在那面大鏡子前把每件都試穿一遍。

sentences.en: I picked out a new backpack for school, plain and simple, while William chose one covered in cartoon dinosaurs, which honestly suited him perfectly.
sentences.zh: 我挑了一個新書包，簡單樸素，William 卻選了一個印滿卡通恐龍的，老實說，那個超級適合他。

sentences.en: At the store, William tried on a formal little suit for a cousin's upcoming wedding and declared himself "extremely handsome" in the mirror.
sentences.zh: 在店裡，William 試穿了一套要參加表親婚禮的正式小西裝，對著鏡子宣布自己「帥到不行」。

sentences.en: I will admit, in that suit, he did look surprisingly handsome, a fact I am choosing not to tell him directly.
sentences.zh: 我得承認，穿那套西裝的他真的意外地帥，但這個事實我選擇不直接告訴他。

sentences.en: Sophia joined us later and pulled out an old box containing her jewelry: a silver bracelet, a small gold ring, and a necklace with one tiny diamond.
sentences.zh: Sophia 後來也加入我們，拿出一個裝著她首飾的舊盒子：一只銀手環、一枚小小的金戒指，還有一條鑲著一顆小鑽石的項鍊。

sentences.en: "This necklace was my mother's," she said quietly. "One diamond, nothing fancy, but it has outlived three houses and one very ugly couch."
sentences.zh: 「這條項鍊是我媽媽的，」她輕聲說：「就一顆鑽石，沒什麼特別的，但它撐過了三次搬家，還有一張非常醜的沙發。」

sentences.en: She also showed us a pair of small silver earrings she wore at her own wedding, kept safe for over forty years.
sentences.zh: 她還給我們看她自己結婚時戴的一對小小銀耳環，保存了超過四十年。

sentences.en: "One day, this necklace is yours," she told me, and for once I had absolutely no funny comment to make.
sentences.zh: 「總有一天，這條項鍊會是你的，」她跟我說，這次我竟然完全講不出任何好笑的話來回應。

sentences.en: By evening, we had new clothing for the coming season, a donation bag for the ugly old sweater William finally gave up, and one rescued slipper Perry still refuses to let go of.
sentences.zh: 到了晚上，我們有了迎接新一季的新衣服、一袋 William 終於放棄的醜毛衣要捐出去，還有一隻 Perry 死都不肯放手的搶救回來的拖鞋。

sentences.en: Lesson of the day: closet clean-out day is exhausting, but it turns out old clothes and old jewelry both carry more stories than I expected.
sentences.zh: 今日心得：衣櫃清理日很累人，但結果發現，舊衣服和舊首飾裡藏的故事，都比我原本以為的還多。

quiz.question: What does Perry try to steal from the donate pile?
Whose necklace does Sophia say will one day belong to Coco?
options: A necklace from her wedding day.

quiz.question: Why does William refuse to give up his old sweater at first?
options: He says it is his favorite color.
He thinks it is worth a lot of money.
He wants to save it for Perry.
